page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. The configuration provides 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. Memory capacity is 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is categorized as poor, and single-threaded gzip compression is weak. However, multi-core performance scales to average across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is a relative highlight, with the Passmark memory score ranking as strong, though memory bandwidth for large blocks remains in the average tier. Large-model LLM inference is weak, but small and medium model inference speeds fall within the average range.

The c5ad.8xlarge presents a tradeoff between lower single-threaded execution speeds and solid multi-threaded throughput. The inclusion of 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space, which enhances qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring fast temporary storage without the overhead of network-attached volumes. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core processing capabilities, this instance is suited for multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, alongside a bundled 1200 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 10 Gbps. Benchmarks show a tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, but multi-core execution, software compilation, and database operations achieve average ratings. Notably, its Passmark memory score is strong. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its high-density local NVMe storage combined with a balanced 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database applications requiring fast local temporary storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average multi-core and strong memory benchmark performance.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, integrated high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong memory performance.

Includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong memory performance and average multi-core benchmark results
